supervisor.reason: The daemonize watcher root cause is fixed and validated. Linux now prioritizes IN_CLOSE_WRITE and IN_MOVED_TO for prompt readiness.

bootstrap.goal: Fix daemonize so Linux inotify reliably detects prompts after write-close and prioritizes inode readiness events over early create notifications.

tasks.resume_checkpoint: Resume from commit preparation only if this validated Linux inotify watcher fix should be versioned now; otherwise the next work can start from a new daemon slice.

items.text: Phase 1. Inspect the current `daemonize` watcher flow and identify why Linux `inotify` can miss a prompt after the writer closes the file
Phase 2. Design a Linux-first watcher adjustment that prioritizes inode readiness events over early create notifications while preserving moved-in prompt detection
Phase 3. Implement the `inotify` watcher fix so `daemonize` relies on `IN_CLOSE_WRITE` and `IN_MOVED_TO` as the primary readiness signals
Phase 4. Add focused regression tests that reproduce delayed close-write behavior and atomic move-in behavior on Linux
Phase 5. Run targeted daemon validation and sync `.dev` state with the results and any remaining watch-mode risks

next_action: Prepare a commit for the validated Linux inotify watcher fix if requested; otherwise treat the daemon slice as complete.
notes: Treat this file as machine truth and keep Markdown synchronized.
DASHBOARD.md should show the actual progress of the active scope.
PLAN.md should list prompt-derived development items in `[ ] Phase N. <title>` format.
The installed guidance bundle and packaged asset copy should stay synchronized.
Legacy TASKS.md snapshots should be migrated forward into PLAN.md without losing checklist state.
A new prompt-driven scope now targets daemonized prompt watching from JSON configuration.
The repository does not currently contain .dev/PROJECT.md or .dev/ROADMAP.md, so planning is grounded in the direct request, AGENTS.md, current code, and machine state.
Daemon mode needs inotify-first monitoring with a 60-second polling fallback where inode-backed watching is unavailable.
The feature request requires separating dormammu workflow skills from per-phase external agent CLI command configuration.
The daemonize design is captured in docs/daemonize-design.md and has been implemented in the new backend/dormammu/daemon package.
The current user request is a Linux inotify watcher fix for daemonize write-close prompt detection.
Root cause: IN_CREATE caused the watcher to drain readiness events too early when settle_seconds skipped the fresh file.
The fix now prefers IN_CLOSE_WRITE and IN_MOVED_TO as the Linux readiness signals for prompt detection.
Focused validation passed through python3 -m unittest tests.test_daemon.InotifyWatcherTests tests.test_daemon.DaemonRunnerTests tests.test_daemon.DaemonConfigTests.
